flashcards.Back: CRM stands for Crew Resource Management.
The effective use of all available resources.
It utilizes human, hardware, and information resources.
Involves the effective utilization of human resources.
Involves the effective utilization of hardware resources.
Involves the effective utilization of info resources.
VO stands for Visual Observer.
To help the RPIC maintain situational awareness.
To help maintain overall team performance.
RPIC stands for Remote Pilot in Command.
The Visual Observer directly assists the RPIC.
The VO helps the RPIC maintain awareness of the situation.
The VO is critical in helping maintain team performance.
Required among all crew members for safe operations.
Includes the VO and the person at the controls.
Must be effective to ensure situational awareness.
Effective communication must be maintained with the person at the controls.
Must check battery levels before every flight.
A mandatory part of the pre-flight inspection.
CS stands for Control Station.
The Control Station battery level must be checked pre-flight.
Ensures both the aircraft and CS have sufficient battery levels.
Elements like fatigue, stress, and dehydration that impact safety.
Fatigue is a physiological factor that degrades operational safety.
Stress is a physiological condition that negatively impacts safety.
Dehydration acts as a physiological factor affecting flight safety.
Include fatigue, stress, and dehydration affecting the crew.
The RPIC must properly allocate tasks to individuals.
Ensures individuals are assigned appropriate duties by the RPIC.
Must be properly allocated by the RPIC to crew members.
The RPIC must actively avoid work overloads.
The RPIC must avoid work overloads for themselves.
The RPIC must prevent work overloads for others.
Falls directly on the Remote Pilot in Command.
It is a key component of team environment operations.
Heavily rely on situational awareness to function safely.
Situational awareness is essential for safe team operations.
Effectively using physical drone equipment and the Control Station.
Effectively utilizing weather data, airspace info, and flight details.
Working together to maintain team performance and awareness.
Must have effective communication with the rest of the crew.
Confirming the Control Station is powered and functioning.
Verifying the drone's battery is fully charged for operation.
Required to prevent physiological impacts on flight safety.
Necessary to ensure the RPIC and crew operate safely.
Critical for mitigating physiological factors during operations.
Involves allocating tasks and preventing work overloads.
The RPIC delegates duties to specific individuals.
Achieved through situational awareness and effective communication.
Using human, hardware, and info resources effectively via CRM.
